title: salvia pratensis subsp. pratensis
intro: Salvia pratensis subsp. pratensis, also known as common meadow sage or meadow sage, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Salvia pratensis subsp. pratensis include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cognitive function, and soothe respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used in folk medicine and herbalism.
